name: gratttool
summary: A full-featured Rust reimplementation of the deprecated BlueZ gatttool utility for interacting with Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) devices via GATT (Generic Attribute Profile).

documentation.readme: A full-featured Rust reimplementation of the deprecated BlueZ gatttool utility for interacting with Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) devices via GATT (Generic Attribute Profile).
usage_sections: Produces character-for-character identical output to the original gatttool
Uses the modern BlueZ D-Bus API instead of deprecated raw sockets
Is written in safe Rust with no C dependencies beyond libdbus
Supports both non-interactive (one-shot) and interactive (shell) modes
Adds enhanced features: --scan device discovery, --enumerate table view, --bdaddr MAC spoofing, --mtu configuration, ASCII string writes, and hidden notification capture

arguments.name: -A
arguments.description: Output read/notification values as ASCII (. for non-printable)

arguments.name: --addr-type
arguments.description: Set LE address type. Default: public [default: public]

arguments.name: --ascii
arguments.description: Output read/notification values as ASCII (. for non-printable)

arguments.name: -coded
arguments.description: green for strong signal (>= -50 dBm), yellow for medium (>= -70), orange for weak (>= -85), and red for very weak.

arguments.name: --enumerate
arguments.description: Enumerate all services, characteristics, and values in a table view

arguments.name: -prone
arguments.description: forgetting -n adds a trailing 0a newline byte):

arguments.name: --scan
arguments.description: Scan for nearby BLE devices (duration in seconds, default 10)

arguments.name: -t
arguments.description: Set LE address type. Default: public [default: public]

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

examples: sudo apt install -y bluetooth bluez libdbus-1-3

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with yohanes/ble-security-research (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with ukBaz/python-bluezero (score=9); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with tsarpaul/ble-research (score=9); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: A full-featured Rust reimplementation of the deprecated BlueZ gatttool utility for interacting with Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) devices via GATT (Generic Attribute Profile).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A full-featured Rust reimplementation of the deprecated BlueZ gatttool utility for interacting with Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) devices via GATT (Generic Attribute Profile).
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `sudo apt install -y bluetooth bluez libdbus-1-3`. Further invocations from upstream: `curl -fsSL -o gratttool \`; `$KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. A full-featured Rust reimplementation of the deprecated BlueZ gatttool utility for interacting with Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) devices via GATT (Generic Attribute Profile).

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Bluetooth, use gratttool because A full-featured Rust reimplementation of the deprecated BlueZ gatttool utility for interacting with Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) devices via GATT (Generic Attribu.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo apt install -y bluetooth bluez libdbus-1-3` as documented upstream for gratttool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `curl -fsSL -o gratttool \` as documented upstream for gratttool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `$KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for gratttool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo mv gratttool /usr/local/bin/gratttool` as documented upstream for gratttool.
When chaining Bluetooth tools, pair gratttool with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `gratttool --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: sudo apt install -y bluetooth bluez libdbus-1-3
